title: sources registry
description: Citation-key -> bibliographic entry. A resolvable identifier (link / doi / urn) is STRONGLY preferred so each cited number is traceable (FAIR: Findable + Accessible) — the validator WARNS when a referenced source lacks one or is unverified. It is not a hard schema error, because legitimate sources (e.g. unarchived student theses, internal logbooks) may have no public identifier and must still be registrable and honestly flagged rather than hidden.

link.description: Resolvable permalink to the source itself (prefer an institutional-repository permalink, e.g. FreiDok).

urn.description: Persistent URN (used where no DOI was minted).

pdf.description: Direct PDF URL, if openly available.

metadata_source.description: Secondary record that confirms the source exists when the source itself is not archived (NOT the source itself).

archived.description: Whether a public archival copy of the source exists.

verified.description: Whether the identifier has been confirmed to resolve (HTTP 200 / DOI resolution).
